title: Cyclosorus porphyricola
taxonomy: Cyclosorus porphyricola is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is native to tropical Asia and belongs to the genus Cyclosorus. The classification of Cyclosorus porphyricola includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Pteridophyta, and class as Polypodiopsida. Further classification includes the order as Polypodiales, family as Thelypteridaceae, genus as Cyclosorus, and species as C. porphyricola. It is a small to medium-sized fern with delicate fronds.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. intro: Cyclosorus porphyricola, also known as the Asian cyclosorus or Chinese lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus porphyricola include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections and fever.
